symptom: TDO always returns 0x00000000, IDCODE reads as 0x00000000
root_cause: CPLD version 0xFFFE — TDO path through CPLD is dead, cable cannot read back
lesson: If IDCODE is all-zeros, check CPLD version first — it's a hardware path issue, not a software bug

action: Load FX2 firmware via fxload to switch PID 0x0013 -> 0x0008
symptom: Cable initially shows as PID 0x0013 (unconfigured FX2)
root_cause: FX2 needs firmware upload on every USB replug — no onboard flash
lesson: Always run fxload with -d (lowercase) flag before any JTAG operation. Use -D for device path.

action: Test UART echo via /dev/tty.usbserial-1120 at 115200 baud
symptom: No echo response from FPGA, minicom shows sent chars but nothing received
root_cause: UART headers on QMTech board are NOT soldered — physical connection absent
lesson: UART requires soldered pin headers on J-series connectors. No headers = no signal.

action: Identify U2 CPLD pinout on Platform Cable USB II board
symptom: Need to understand signal routing between FX2, CPLD, and JTAG header
root_cause: U2 is XC2C256 CPLD that routes TDI/TDO/TMS/TCK between FX2 USB chip and JTAG header
lesson: Platform Cable USB II has 3 chips: FX2 (USB), CPLD (routing), and JTAG header. CPLD is the bridge.

symptom: openFPGALoader returns error: cable 'xpc' not supported
root_cause: openFPGALoader does not support Xilinx Platform Cable USB II (xpc) — only FTDI-based cables
lesson: NEVER use openFPGALoader with --cable xpc. Use native jtag_program/jtag_switcher instead.

action: Order replacement equipment: new Platform Cable USB II + soldering kit for UART headers
symptom: Current cable has dead CPLD, UART headers not soldered
root_cause: Hardware limitations cannot be fixed with software
lesson: When hardware is defective, order replacement — don't waste time on software workarounds

action: Compare original xpc.c with refactored version — same CPLD version bug
symptom: Both original and refactored jtag_program read CPLD version 0xFFFE
root_cause: Bug is in hardware (CPLD), not in software — both code paths use identical USB control transfers
lesson: If original and refactored code produce same bug, the problem is below the software layer

root_cause: 0xFFFE means CPLD GPIF bus is stuck high (all 1s except LSB) — CPLD is not responding to version query, TDO routing through CPLD is dead
lesson: CPLD version 0xFFFE = dead CPLD. TDI works (writing succeeds) but TDO is dead (reading fails). Don't debug software for this.
